refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall enable achieving the customer-defined productivity target by optimizing equipment operation and task execution efficiency.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is broad and outcome-based; refining it into sub-FRs focused on key productivity drivers (equipment operation efficiency and task execution efficiency) allows targeted design and verification.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure productivity metrics (m³/h or ton/h) during representative operations and verify that optimized equipment operation and task execution contribute to meeting or exceeding the target.
sub_frs: FR1: Optimize equipment operation to maximize throughput.
FR2: Optimize task execution efficiency to minimize idle and transition times.

candidate_dps: DP1: Implement advanced vehicle control algorithms to maximize operational throughput.
DP2: Implement intelligent task scheduling and coordination to reduce idle and transition times.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1: Implement advanced vehicle control algorithms to maximize operational throughput.

potential_sources: Interaction between vehicle control and task scheduling may cause coupling.
coupling_check.notes: DP1 and DP2 are logically separable; DP1 focuses on vehicle control, DP2 on task scheduling. Selected DP1 is decoupled from task scheduling logic.

assumptions_risks: Assumes vehicle control algorithms can be independently optimized without immediate task scheduling integration.
Risk that ignoring task scheduling may limit overall productivity gains.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider integrating task scheduling DP in next decomposition level to address potential coupling.

im_boundary.name: Vehicle
im_boundary.scope: Physical and software components responsible for vehicle control and operation.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Vehicle control module outputs operational data for monitoring and analysis.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives task commands and schedules from higher-level coordination modules.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Communication channel for remote control and data exchange.

module_containment_scores.notes: Broad scope; contains many functions beyond vehicle control, leading to diffusion.
High containment of vehicle control DP; low diffusion risk.
Primarily focused on remote control, less relevant for vehicle control DP.
Safety functions are separate; minimal containment of vehicle control DP.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Vehicle module is the best architectural boundary to contain the advanced vehicle control algorithms, minimizing diffusion and enabling clear ownership.
containment_risks: Potential interface complexity with Teleoperation and NGHE Solution modules.
Risk of duplicated control logic if coordination with Teleoperation is not well managed.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Define clear interface contracts with Teleoperation and NGHE Solution to mitigate diffusion risks.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The selected DP logically satisfies the refined FR by focusing on vehicle control to optimize throughput, with clear sub-FRs and verification approach.

diffusion_risks: Possible diffusion of control logic if interfaces with Teleoperation and NGHE Solution are not well defined.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Vehicle module shows high containment score and low diffusion score, supporting good architectural packaging.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed by monitoring vehicle operational data via Telemetry and measuring productivity metrics.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of vehicle control DP to Vehicle module with defined external interfaces.

recommended_revisions: Refine interface definitions between Vehicle and Teleoperation modules.
Plan next level decomposition to address task scheduling DP and integration.

decision.justification: Current level decomposition is consistent, with clear FR refinement, DP selection, and IM assignment. Architectural packaging is sound with manageable risks.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Optimize equipment operation to maximize throughput.
FR2: Optimize task execution efficiency to minimize idle and transition times.

revise.why: No immediate revision needed; proceed to next decomposition level.

assumptions: Vehicle control algorithms can be developed independently of task scheduling initially.
Interfaces with Teleoperation and NGHE Solution will be clearly defined.
open_risks: Potential diffusion of control logic across modules if interfaces are not well managed.
Risk that ignoring task scheduling DP at this level may limit productivity gains.
evidence_needed: Performance data from vehicle control algorithms during representative operations.
Interface specification documents between Vehicle and Teleoperation modules.
